markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose backend APIs for claim lookup, conflict review, evidence trace, graph neighborhood, and repair queues. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: MemoryGraphBackendApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MemoryGraphBackendApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified ff67de1c; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-126 requires Handshake-native backend navigation APIs for the memory graph (claim lookup, conflict review/repair queue, evidence/fact trace, graph neighborhood, visual-debug) over PostgreSQL/EventLedger with attributable typed receipts. The committed code (api/knowledge_memory.rs) implements all five read-only endpoints over the shared postgres_pool, enforces backend-nav identity headers (400 otherwise), and emits a typed KNOWLEDGE_RETRIEVAL_TRACE_RECORDED EventLedger receipt per query, with a real route-level HTTP integration test against managed PostgreSQL. This matches spec 2.3.13.11 (line 2717).
findings: api/knowledge_memory.rs:64-78 builds a real routes(state) Router exposing all five MT-required endpoints: claims/:id, conflicts (repair queue), facts/:id (trace), entities/:id/neighborhood, visual-debug; merged into the live app router at api/mod.rs:18,43,68.

Backend-navigation receipt law enforced: nav_context (lines 134-168) requires x-hsk-actor-id, x-hsk-kernel-task-run-id, x-hsk-session-run-id (returns 400 bad_request if absent); record_nav_receipt (lines 171-201) appends a typed KernelEventType::KnowledgeRetrievalTraceRecorded EventLedger event carrying actor/session/correlation + query. Matches spec 2.3.13.11 line 2717 (actor id, session id, correlation id, target authority ref, intended operation, typed receipt).

All durable reads go through real PostgresDatabase/KnowledgeStore impls, not implementer mocks: list_knowledge_claim_span_ids, list_knowledge_claim_conflicts, get_knowledge_claim, list_knowledge_edges_for_entity, list_knowledge_edge_span_ids all have real impls at src/storage/knowledge.rs:4220-4642; get_memory_fact/get_memory_fact_by_claim/create_memory_fact real at src/storage/knowledge_memory.rs:693-735. Conflict queue uses a direct SQL join over knowledge_claim_conflicts/knowledge_claims (lines 289-308).

sub_rule_2 PASS: test knowledge_memory_api_tests.rs drives the actual Axum routes over a loopback listener against REAL managed PostgreSQL via MemoryFixture::setup -> knowledge_pg() auto-discovery with isolated schema + full migration chain (fixtures line 8-9, 43-44: 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks'). Asserts 400-without-headers (line 215), receipt event ids on every response, claim/conflict/fact/neighborhood/visual-debug shapes, and lifecycle_state=conflicted.

sub_rule_1 PASS: grep of api/knowledge_memory.rs and visual_debug.rs found zero todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT'/Unavailable placeholders reachable from the proof path.
sub_rule_3 PASS: claimed_by/completed_by are null; ready_by=KERNEL_BUILDER (READY_FOR_VALIDATION). No self-certified COMPLETED transition, and proof is real regardless.

No forbidden surfaces: no SQLite, Docker, external search engine, or sidecar-paper authority; all authority is PostgreSQL/EventLedger. The single commit ff67de1c bundles MT-126 (api) and MT-127 (visual_debug payload); MT-126 scope is cleanly the api file + its test, within packet allowed_paths (src/backend/handshake_core, tests).
Aside (non-blocking, governance paperwork): MT contract still cites v02.192 anchors and lifecycle.status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION; expected during governance wind-down, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
